title: Business AIs vs Meta AI Business Assistant are distinct products
insight: Meta's Business AIs and Meta AI Business Assistant are very different despite similar names. Business AIs are sales concierge agents present on websites, in ads, or in messaging (WhatsApp/Messenger) — essentially white-glove AI that answers consumer questions about the brand/product. Meta AI Business Assistant is an AI-powered advisor embedded in Ads Manager that helps marketers find better performance or fix account issues with personalized recommendations. Business AIs are customer-facing; the Business Assistant is marketer-facing.
raw_excerpt: Despite similar sounding names these products are very, very different. They're distinct products for quite different audiences and roles. Business AIs is essentially a sales concierge... Meta AI Business Assistant really helps marketers find better performance or sort out and fix account issues with personalized AI recommendations integrated via AI-driven chat within Meta's ad tools.
source_title: Mobile Dev Memo S6E18: Meta's Business AI and the ad-product divide

title: Business AIs reveal consumers ask extremely basic product questions
insight: Early testing of Meta Business AIs shows that consumers frequently ask incredibly basic questions that businesses assume everyone already knows — like asking 'is this a blusher?' on a page where the product is obviously a blusher. Businesses consistently overestimate baseline consumer understanding. This insight suggests that ad creative and landing pages should not assume product knowledge, and that AI agents can surface these knowledge gaps that no human team would catch.
raw_excerpt: Many of the questions that consumers are asking these business AIs are incredibly basic. The CMO of ELF was telling me that as basic as, 'is this a blusher?' on a page where most people would know that's a blusher. But I think as humans, we often set the baseline understanding significantly higher than reality.
source_title: Mobile Dev Memo S6E18: Meta's Business AI and the ad-product divide
actionable_steps: Deploy conversational AI on product pages to surface what questions users actually ask
Use those questions to inform ad creative messaging — don't assume product knowledge
Audit landing pages for assumed knowledge gaps that may block conversion

title: Business AIs surface demand signals like bestseller collections
insight: A retail brand using Meta Business AIs discovered that prospective customers were repeatedly asking about bestsellers, even though no bestseller collection existed on their site. They built one as a result. AI agents can reveal latent consumer demand that traditional analytics miss, because they capture intent expressed in natural language before the user bounces. This has direct implications for product merchandising and conversion optimization.
raw_excerpt: Another retail brand we work with found the prospective clients were asking about bestsellers all the time, even though a bestsellers collection didn't exist within that individual business. But now, as a result of the questions, they've built one out.
source_title: Mobile Dev Memo S6E18: Meta's Business AI and the ad-product divide
actionable_steps: Implement conversational AI to capture user questions and aggregate them for product insights
Identify high-frequency questions that indicate missing content or features on your site
Build landing pages or collections based on actual demand signals from AI conversations

title: Creative differentiation by format, theme, and concept unlocks unique reach
insight: When partners find a creative that works, they invariably create 1000 iterations of that one idea — effectively reaching the same small audience repeatedly. Meta's system is good at matching creative to person, but it needs differentiation across formats (use all surfaces), themes, and concepts to unlock unique reach. Advertisers should monitor 'unique people reached' and its correlation with creative diversity. Iterations fight fatigue; new concepts unlock new audiences.
raw_excerpt: When partners find a creative that works, they invariably do exactly the wrong thing, which is they create 1000 iterations on that 1 idea, which effectively means that they're reaching the same small audience of people time and time again. What we need is this notion of differentiation — of formats, of themes, and of concepts. If you do that, you can actually reach many more people more efficiently.
source_title: Mobile Dev Memo S6E18: Meta's Business AI and the ad-product divide
actionable_steps: Audit creative portfolio for genuine concept diversity vs iterations on one winning idea
Diversify across formats (Reels, Feed, Stories), themes, and concepts — not just variations
Track unique people reached alongside creative diversity metrics
Use Gen AI tools to produce format-specific variations affordably rather than only iterating on winners

title: ASC delivers 22% more ROAS; $1 in returns $4.52
insight: Advantage+ Shopping Campaigns with AI-driven targeting features deliver on average 22% more ROAS than when those features are not enabled. Meta reports that $1 put into ASC returns $4.52. ASC automates targeting, bidding, and creative selection, running billions of permutations to find performance pockets no manual team could replicate. This makes a strong case for advertisers to migrate to ASC for performance marketing.
raw_excerpt: When you turn on the product's AI-driven targeting features, on average, they deliver 22% more ROAS than when you don't turn them on. When you put a dollar into the ASC AI machine, it spits out $4.52.
source_title: Mobile Dev Memo S6E18: Meta's Business AI and the ad-product divide
actionable_steps: Enable ASC with full AI-driven targeting features rather than restricting them
Compare ROAS with and without ASC AI features enabled to validate lift for your account
Let ASC handle targeting/bidding/creative permutations rather than manually over-constraining

title: Full funnel personalization is the next frontier — collapse the funnel with AI
insight: Personalizing ad creative at the top of funnel is only half the value if users land on the same generic landing page. The real opportunity is collapsing the funnel: an individualized creative leads to a personalized post-click experience informed by what the user engaged with. Business AIs enable this by receiving the user with context and answering their specific questions, taking them further down the funnel. This is especially powerful for higher-consideration purchases and opens digital marketing for businesses that previously struggled with online conversion.
raw_excerpt: If you take that personalization on platform to the limit, you got an individualized creative... and then they click through and they see the same landing page that everyone else is seeing. So there's an obvious next step here... The opportunity here is enormous if we can get this right.
source_title: Mobile Dev Memo S6E18: Meta's Business AI and the ad-product divide
actionable_steps: Implement post-click personalization that reflects the ad creative the user engaged with
Use UTM parameters or deep link data to customize landing page experience
Consider Business AIs as a conversion optimization layer between ad click and purchase

title: Value optimization is Meta's most performant mode for high-variance conversion values
insight: Meta's value optimization goes beyond traditional conversion optimization to optimize for the highest-ROAS conversions. It's most performant when a business has many conversions of vastly different values — e.g., optimizing for large basket sizes. Meta is expanding into profit optimization, where a business can signal that a $10 product is better for their bottom line than a $30 product. Value optimization now represents a large percentage of Meta's performance marketing and is growing.
raw_excerpt: Value optimization is the most performant way to go in instances where a business has a lot of conversions of vastly different values. We're also expanding into things like profit... We're essentially telling our partners, you tell us exactly what you want to do with your campaign and our ad system will go and optimize for that specific outcome.
source_title: Mobile Dev Memo S6E18: Meta's Business AI and the ad-product divide
actionable_steps: If your conversions have widely varying values, switch from conversion volume optimization to value optimization
Pass accurate conversion values back to Meta including profit signals where possible
Test ROAS-optimized vs volume-optimized campaigns to find which drives better bottom-line outcomes

title: Catalog ads are Meta's lowest-funnel product and work even for small catalogs
insight: Catalog ads are the lowest funnel product in Meta's suite and perform extremely well because Meta can predict with high confidence which products an individual consumer will be interested in. Even small businesses with catalogs of 100 products or fewer should lean into catalog advertising. It showcases dynamic product selections optimized not just for person-ad fit but person-product fit in context.
raw_excerpt: Catalog advertising is the lowest funnel product that we have within the Metasuite. So it performs incredibly well, because we can predict with a high degree of confidence, the product or products an individual consumer is gonna be interested in. So even a small business with a catalog of 100 products or less has an opportunity to lean in here.
source_title: Mobile Dev Memo S6E18: Meta's Business AI and the ad-product divide
actionable_steps: Set up a product catalog on Meta even with fewer than 100 products
Use catalog ads for bottom-funnel campaigns to leverage Meta's product-level prediction
Ensure product feed data is complete and accurate — this directly impacts ad quality

title: Video generation tools are a game-changer for SMBs in short-form video era
insight: Over 2 million advertisers are already using Meta's video generation features. The tool transforms multiple static images into dynamic multi-scene videos with text overlays and music. This is a game-changer because short-form video is the preeminent content format on Meta's apps, so video ads native to that environment vastly outperform static images. Previously, video production was prohibitively expensive for small businesses — now they can participate in the highest-engagement format.
raw_excerpt: 2 million advertisers are already using our video generation features alone... We have our video generation beta which allows you to transform multiple static images into dynamic multi-scene videos with text overlays and music. Short form video is the preeminent content inside of our apps and services.
source_title: Mobile Dev Memo S6E18: Meta's Business AI and the ad-product divide
actionable_steps: Use Meta's video generation tools to convert existing static product images into short-form video ads
Prioritize short-form video format for ad creative as it matches the dominant content consumption behavior
If you're a small business running only statics, test Meta's auto-generated video to unlock video placement reach

title: Gen AI creative tools expand ad economy participation, not just volume
insight: AI creative tools don't just help existing advertisers produce more — they bring entirely new advertisers into the ad economy. Many businesses had zero capability to create ads or understand campaign optimization. The expansion is dual-axis: existing advertisers converting and spending more, plus net-new advertisers participating for the first time. The ad economy participation expansion from AI tools is underrecognized as a value driver. Small businesses adopt these tools faster than large enterprises.
raw_excerpt: Having this capability given to you is allowing advertisers to advertise for the first time because there's a lot of them that had no capability to come up with an ad or even just the optimization... I think that ad economy participation piece, that expansion is not recognized as much.
source_title: Mobile Dev Memo S6E18: Meta's Business AI and the ad-product divide

title: Andromeda updates are not linked to performance issues — creative ranking is distinct
insight: Simon Whitcomb states with absolute confidence that no recent Andromeda updates are linked to any advertiser performance issues. Andromeda is efficient at scaling ad selections from a growing volume of ad creatives, but it is distinct from creative diversification which is impacted by creative ranking. Advertisers should not blame Andromeda for performance drops — the root cause is more likely creative, signal, or targeting related.
raw_excerpt: I can say with absolute confidence that no recent Andromeda updates are linked to any performance issues, if anybody's still wondering about that. While Andromeda is efficient at scaling ad selections from a growing volume of ad creatives, it is quite distinct from creative diversification, which is directly impacted by creative ranking.
source_title: Mobile Dev Memo S6E18: Meta's Business AI and the ad-product divide
actionable_steps: Don't attribute performance issues to Andromeda — investigate creative quality, signal, and targeting first
Understand that Andromeda handles ad selection at scale while creative ranking determines which specific ads win

title: Entertainment/streaming industry unlock is pivoting to LTV across business units
insight: Entertainment companies have become strong at customer acquisition but the big unlock is pivoting to an LTV lens that connects their streaming, movie, theme park, and TV arms. A streaming subscriber may not be profitable on their own, but their LTV impact across other business units could justify much higher acquisition spend. Companies that connect these dots and optimize for cross-unit LTV will outspend and outgrow competitors still optimizing for single-channel ROAS.
raw_excerpt: The big unlock for that industry is going to be when they pivot to LTV... understanding the LTV relationship between each one of those businesses, understanding it might not be as profitable to onboard a streaming service, but the LTV impact to the other business units could be that great.
source_title: Mobile Dev Memo S6E18: Meta's Business AI and the ad-product divide
actionable_steps: Map LTV across all product/service lines to understand true user value beyond single-channel attribution
If running multiple subscription tiers or product lines, model cross-sell and reactivation value into LTV
Use cross-unit LTV to justify higher acquisition bids on channels that bring high-value multi-product users

title: Large enterprises are slow to adopt AI tools due to workflow rigidity, not technology fear
insight: Large businesses aren't avoiding AI tools because they fear the technology — they're slow because adopting AI requires reimagining internal workflows, not just swapping in a component. Legal sign-off processes, creative approval chains, and established operating procedures all need restructuring. Large enterprises focus on what could go wrong rather than what could go right. Meanwhile, small and medium businesses lean in faster and gain first-mover advantage. Marketers should be experimenting with Gen AI creative tools now, even if not running results in production.
raw_excerpt: It's not so much about adopting the technology or being prepared to do that. It's really about changing how you operate as a large enterprise. And that can be quite hard for those large businesses... It's imperative for all advertisers of all sizes to begin experimenting with Gen AI for creative, even if they don't run the results in production.
source_title: Mobile Dev Memo S6E18: Meta's Business AI and the ad-product divide
actionable_steps: Start experimenting with Gen AI creative tools now, even if not deploying to production yet
Understand the gap between AI output and your production-ready standard — that gap is closing rapidly
Restructure creative workflows around AI capabilities rather than swapping AI into existing processes
